$comment: SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for the tool catalogue. TS packages/shared/src/ops.gen.ts is GENERATED from this (scripts/gen-ops.ts, run via `just gen`); Rust tools/catalogue.rs is VERIFIED against it (cargo test); bridge-request.schema.json's OpArgs must stay the union of these inputSchema properties minus `browser` (enforced by the packages/shared equivalence test). Edit here, then regenerate + test.

tools.name: list_browsers
tools.description: List the browsers currently connected to the bridge. Returns each browser's label and its open-tab count. When more than one browser is connected, every other tool needs a `browser` argument set to one of these labels.

tools.name: tab_list
tools.description: List all open browser tabs. Returns id, title, url, and which is active.

browser.description: Optional: which connected browser to run this on - a label from list_browsers. Required when more than one browser is connected.

tools.name: tab_focus
tools.description: Bring a tab to the foreground (make it active).

browser.description: Optional: which connected browser to run this on - a label from list_browsers. Required when more than one browser is connected.

tools.name: tab_open
tools.uiLabel: 打开新标签页(需白名单)
tools.description: Open a URL in a new tab. The host domain must be in the user's allowlist.

browser.description: Optional: which connected browser to run this on - a label from list_browsers. Required when more than one browser is connected.

tools.name: tab_close
tools.description: Close an http(s) tab after the user approves it in the extension's confirmation window.

browser.description: Optional: which connected browser to run this on - a label from list_browsers. Required when more than one browser is connected.

tools.name: page_snapshot
tools.description: Capture the active tab's interactive elements as an accessibility-style tree. Each node has a stable `ref` (e.g. "e3"), a role, an accessible name, and a fallback CSS selector. Use the `ref` in page_click/page_fill when possible.

browser.description: Optional: which connected browser to run this on - a label from list_browsers. Required when more than one browser is connected.

tools.name: page_click
tools.description: Click an element on the active tab. Prefer passing `ref` (from page_snapshot); fall back to `selector`. Clicking a submit button or a link triggers a user-confirmation prompt.

browser.description: Optional: which connected browser to run this on - a label from list_browsers. Required when more than one browser is connected.

tools.name: page_fill
tools.description: Type a value into a form field on the active tab. Prefer `ref`; fall back to `selector`. Password fields are masked in logs/history.

value.description: Text to type into the field

browser.description: Optional: which connected browser to run this on - a label from list_browsers. Required when more than one browser is connected.

tools.name: page_text
tools.description: Return the visible text content of the active tab (sensitive fields masked).

browser.description: Optional: which connected browser to run this on - a label from list_browsers. Required when more than one browser is connected.

tools.name: page_screenshot
tools.description: Capture the visible viewport of the active tab as a PNG (base64).

browser.description: Optional: which connected browser to run this on - a label from list_browsers. Required when more than one browser is connected.

tools.name: page_scroll
tools.description: Scroll the active tab. Pass `direction` (up|down|top|bottom) or `pixels`.

direction.description: One of: up, down, top, bottom

pixels.description: Number of pixels to scroll (positive = down)

browser.description: Optional: which connected browser to run this on - a label from list_browsers. Required when more than one browser is connected.

tools.name: page_wait_for
tools.description: Wait until a condition is met on the active tab, or until timeout. One of: `selector` exists, `text` appears, or `nav` waits for page load completion.

selector.description: Wait for this selector to match an element

text.description: Wait for this text to appear in the page

browser.description: Optional: which connected browser to run this on - a label from list_browsers. Required when more than one browser is connected.

tools.name: page_eval
tools.description: HIGH RISK — execute arbitrary JavaScript on the active tab. EVERY call shows the user the full code in a confirmation prompt and waits for approval; there is no silent same-origin grace window for eval, so every page_eval re-prompts. The return value is masked (JWT / long hex / long numbers / token-like strings) by default. This is the most powerful tool: prefer page_click / page_fill / page_snapshot whenever possible, and only use page_eval when those cannot achieve the goal (custom events, reading framework state, SPA routing, canvas/WebGL, etc.). Code runs in the page's global scope, wrapped as `async`, so you can `await` and `return` a value. Async results are awaited. Errors are returned as {name, message}.

browser.description: Optional: which connected browser to run this on - a label from list_browsers. Required when more than one browser is connected.

tools.name: page_snapshot_precise
tools.description: Like page_snapshot, but uses Chrome's debugger (CDP Accessibility.getFullAXTree) to capture the AUTHORITATIVE accessibility tree — accurate for shadow DOM and complex ARIA where the content-script approximation misses. The user is warned first (a brief on-page notice); Chrome then shows a 'Started debugging this browser' banner on all tabs for ~1 second while the snapshot is taken, then it disappears. Cannot run on chrome:// / web store pages, or tabs with DevTools open. Refs use a 'p' prefix (p1, p2...) and work with page_click / page_fill unchanged. Use this when page_snapshot misses elements or roles look wrong.

frameId.description: Optional: limit to a specific frame's tree

browser.description: Optional: which connected browser to run this on - a label from list_browsers. Required when more than one browser is connected.

tools.name: cookie_get
tools.description: Read cookies for the active tab (or a url/domain you specify). Includes httpOnly cookies (the main reason to use this over document.cookie). Scoped to hosts in the user's allowlist — unauthorized hosts silently return nothing. Read-only; there is no cookie_set (writing httpOnly cookies is a session-fixation risk). Values are masked (JWT / long hex / long numbers) before being returned. If you omit url/domain/name, cookies for the active tab's URL are returned.

domain.description: Match this domain and its subdomains

url.description: Return cookies that would be sent to this URL

browser.description: Optional: which connected browser to run this on - a label from list_browsers. Required when more than one browser is connected.

tools.name: storage_get
tools.description: Read the page's localStorage or sessionStorage (where frameworks like Auth0 / NextAuth / Firebase store tokens). Must run on the active tab; same-origin only (cross-origin iframes are not readable). Pass `key` to fetch one entry, or omit it to dump all entries (capped at 500). Values are ALWAYS masked (JWT / long hex / long numbers) — this masking is not toggleable. Read-only.

key.description: Specific key to read; omit for all entries

browser.description: Optional: which connected browser to run this on - a label from list_browsers. Required when more than one browser is connected.

tools.name: page_navigate
tools.uiLabel: 导航当前标签页(需白名单)
tools.description: Navigate the active tab to an http(s) URL. The host domain must be in the user's allowlist. This loads in the CURRENT tab (use tab_open to open a new tab instead).

url.description: Absolute http(s) URL to load in the active tab

browser.description: Optional: which connected browser to run this on - a label from list_browsers. Required when more than one browser is connected.

tools.name: page_back
tools.description: Navigate the active tab back one step in its session history (the browser Back button). Errors if there is nothing to go back to.

browser.description: Optional: which connected browser to run this on - a label from list_browsers. Required when more than one browser is connected.

tools.name: page_forward
tools.description: Navigate the active tab forward one step in its session history (the browser Forward button). Errors if there is nothing to go forward to.

browser.description: Optional: which connected browser to run this on - a label from list_browsers. Required when more than one browser is connected.

tools.name: page_reload
tools.description: Reload the active tab (the browser Reload button).

browser.description: Optional: which connected browser to run this on - a label from list_browsers. Required when more than one browser is connected.

tools.name: page_press
tools.description: Send a keyboard key or combo to the active tab, e.g. "Enter", "Escape", or "Control+A". Dispatched as a synthetic keyboard event to the focused element, so page JavaScript handlers see it; the event is not trusted, so it may not trigger a native default action such as submitting a form (use page_click on the submit control for that). Every press shows a user-confirmation prompt (in the extension's own window), because a keypress can submit or trigger an action.

keys.description: A single key or combo, e.g. "Enter", "Escape", "Tab", "a", or "Control+A". Modifiers: Control, Shift, Alt, Meta.

browser.description: Optional: which connected browser to run this on - a label from list_browsers. Required when more than one browser is connected.

tools.name: page_hover
tools.description: Move the pointer over an element on the active tab (dispatches pointerover / mouseover / mouseenter / mousemove), revealing hover menus or tooltips. Prefer `ref` (from page_snapshot); fall back to `selector`.

browser.description: Optional: which connected browser to run this on - a label from list_browsers. Required when more than one browser is connected.

tools.name: page_select
tools.description: Choose an option in a <select> drop-down on the active tab. Prefer `ref` (from page_snapshot); fall back to `selector`. `value` matches an option by its value attribute, or by its visible text when no value matches. Fires input and change events so frameworks react. Shows a user-confirmation prompt (in the extension's own window), because it changes form state.

ref.description: Element ref from page_snapshot for the <select>

value.description: Option to choose: its value attribute, or its visible text

browser.description: Optional: which connected browser to run this on - a label from list_browsers. Required when more than one browser is connected.

tools.name: console_get
tools.description: Return recent console output from the active tab, captured via Chrome's debugger. Includes browser-logged entries (network, security, and deprecation warnings, which the debugger replays on attach) plus any console.* calls and uncaught errors during the short capture window; console.* output produced before this call is generally not available. Values are masked (JWT / long hex / long numbers / token-like strings), because console lines can carry tokens. Attaching briefly shows the 'Started debugging this browser' banner. `limit` caps the number of entries returned (default 100).

browser.description: Optional: which connected browser to run this on - a label from list_browsers. Required when more than one browser is connected.

tools.name: page_handle_dialog
tools.uiLabel: 响应页面对话框(需开启)
tools.description: Respond to a JavaScript dialog (alert / confirm / prompt) on the active tab: `action` is "accept" or "dismiss", with optional `promptText` for a prompt(). Uses Chrome's debugger (CDP Page.handleJavaScriptDialog). HIGH RISK, because accepting a dialog can confirm a destructive action, so this tool is OFF by default and must be enabled in the extension settings. A dialog blocks the page, so the confirmation cannot be shown in-page; the settings opt-in is the gate. Chrome needs the debugger attached when the dialog opens for it to be handleable (turn on CDP mode), otherwise the dialog may not be capturable.

promptText.description: Text to enter for a prompt() before accepting

browser.description: Optional: which connected browser to run this on - a label from list_browsers. Required when more than one browser is connected.

tools.name: page_upload
tools.uiLabel: 上传本地文件(极高危,需开启)
tools.description: CRITICAL RISK - attach a LOCAL file from the user's disk to a file input (<input type=file>) on the active tab so the page can upload it. `selector` targets the file input; `path` is the absolute local file path. This can exfiltrate private local files to a web page, so it is OFF by default (enable in the extension settings) and EVERY call shows a user-confirmation prompt (in the extension's own window) displaying the exact file path before it proceeds. Uses Chrome's debugger (CDP DOM.setFileInputFiles); the 'Started debugging this browser' banner flashes while it runs. Do not use this unless the user explicitly asked to upload that specific file.

selector.description: CSS selector for the <input type=file> element

path.description: Absolute local filesystem path of the file to attach

browser.description: Optional: which connected browser to run this on - a label from list_browsers. Required when more than one browser is connected.
